level_signals: Track record of leading products from 0→1 and through scale
Drive system architecture decisions with engineering

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Deep technical product experience: building highly technical products, ideally across infrastructure, platform, or developer tooling
Strong systems intuition: Ability to reason about distributed systems, scalability, latency, and reliability from first principles
End-to-end product ownership: Track record of leading products from 0→1 and through scale, working closely with engineering and cross-functional teams
Excellent collaboration and communication: Ability to work deeply with engineers while clearly articulating tradeoffs and decisions across stakeholders
Comfort with ambiguity and pace: Experience operating in fast-moving environments, building new systems with incomplete information

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with distributed systems, infrastructure, or platform engineering (compute, storage, networking, orchestration)
Experience building developer platforms, APIs, or SDKs
Familiarity with modern AI/ML infrastructure or real-time systems
Some coding experience (e.g., Typescript, Go, Python)

scope.surface: Infrastructure platform powering agent execution — compute, storage, orchestration, networking, developer APIs/SDKs
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS — AI platform for enterprises
primary_metrics_inferred: Developer velocity / time-to-ship for internal teams
core_responsibilities: Define the infrastructure platform: Shape the abstractions that power agent execution—including compute, storage, orchestration, and networking layers—making them scalable, reliable, and developer-friendly.
Own performance, reliability, and scale: Define how Sierra systems handle real-time workloads, high concurrency, and enterprise-grade uptime requirements.
Design developer primitives and tooling: Build the internal and external interfaces (APIs, SDKs, workflows) that engineers use to deploy, monitor, and iterate on agents.
Drive system architecture decisions: Partner closely with engineering to make tradeoffs across consistency, cost, performance, and complexity.
Enable rapid iteration across the company: Ensure infrastructure accelerates product velocity, not slows it down.
Work across the full stack of the platform: Collaborate with product, engineering, and GTM teams to ensure infrastructure supports real customer use cases.

green_flags: Co-founders with exceptional pedigree (Bret Taylor, Clay Bavor) signal strong company credibility
Clear and specific scope — infrastructure PM role with concrete ownership areas
Explicit encouragement to apply even if experience doesn't precisely match
Equity participation mentioned for full-time employees
Comprehensive benefits package including parental leave, fertility benefits, and flexible PTO
red_flags: Primarily in-person in San Francisco — eliminates remote candidates
Work authorization and visa sponsorship policies not stated
No years of experience specified, making seniority bar unclear

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from scope of responsibilities and 0→1 track record requirement)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description — AI platform for businesses)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'primarily an in-person company based in San Francisco')
compensation.equity_form (inferred; JD says 'equity plans' without specifying RSUs vs options)
ambiguities: 'ideally across infrastructure, platform, or developer tooling' in the must-haves section uses 'ideally' which softens it, but it sits in the required 'What you'll bring' section — treated as hard with domain qualifier noted
Visa sponsorship and work authorization are completely unaddressed in the JD
